sourceRef.agent: AlphaEvolve (Google DeepMind, Gemini Flash/Pro + evolutionary loop)
sourceRef.paper: arXiv:2603.09172 — Reinforced Generation of Combinatorial Structures: Ramsey Numbers

statement.en: AlphaEvolve improved the lower bounds of 9 classical Ramsey numbers: R(3,13): 60→61, R(3,18): 99→100, R(4,13): 138→139, R(4,14): 147→148, R(4,15): 158→159, R(4,16): 170→174, R(4,18): 205→209, R(4,19): 213→219, R(4,20): 234→237. Existing best lower bounds for all known-exact Ramsey numbers were also recovered, and the best known lower bounds across many other cases were matched.
statement.ja: AlphaEvolve が 9 個の古典的 Ramsey 数下限を改良: R(3,13) 60→61 / R(3,18) 99→100 / R(4,13-20) 計 7 件. 既知 exact Ramsey 数の最良下限も再現済.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Specific lower bounds verified — TRUE for existence
reiTyping.rationale: Each new R(s,t) bound is a constructive existence proof (an explicit graph witness). The graphs themselves were found by AlphaEvolve evolutionary search and are explicitly published.

reiInterest.lean4Candidate: ★ HIGH — 9 explicit graphs published. Each (s,t) bound formalizable as: ∃ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), (no K_s subgraph) ∧ (no independent set of size t). Mathlib PR candidate.
reiInterest.paperCandidate: ★ Paper 154 candidate: 'Rei formal verification of AlphaEvolve Ramsey graphs (R(4,16)=174 etc.)' — fills the '證明完成器' role per Tao framing.
reiInterest.relationToReiStack: Complements REI-PROVE 5-prover ensemble (STEP 1021/1057-1067) and AlphaEvolve engine local impl (alpha-evolve-engine.ts STEP 717). Rei does NOT need to find the graphs (AlphaEvolve did) — Rei provides the formal verification layer.

cross_refs.mathlib_module_candidate: Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Ramsey (does not yet exist as of 2026-05)

honestScope: Rei has NOT independently verified these graphs (yet). Ingest is a META-DB tier-10 (external-ai) ENTRY for future Rei verification. Pattern 5 prevention: the ramsey-num improvements are CITED, not claimed as Rei output.
